revision.text:
[[en:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[es:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو اوتومورو]][[fr:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[id:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[ja:藤原乙牟漏]][[no:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[sv:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[th:ฟูจิวาระ โนะ โอโตมูโระ]][[tl:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[vi:Fujiwara no Otomuro]][[zh:藤原乙牟漏]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0760|death_date={{death date|0790|4|28}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|qid=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}|children={{ill|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}}; {{ill|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}; {{ill|Koshi-naishinnō|qid=Q11669919|1=Koshi-naishinnō}}|father={{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|qid=Q11623505|1=Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu}}|mother={{ill|Abe no Komina|qid=Q17191701|1=Abe no Komina}}|relatives={{ill|Fujiwara no Moroane|qid=Q5507531|1=Fujiwara no Moroane}}|native_name=藤原 乙牟漏}} {{family name hatnote|Fujiwara|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox royalty | burial_place = Nagaoka Mausoleum, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mukō|en|Mukō|qid=Q850640|lt=Mukō|1=Mukō}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | consort = yes | name = Fujiwara no Otomuro
藤原乙牟漏 | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 23 May 783 – 790 | birth_date = 760 | birth_place = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | death_date = 28 April 790 | death_place = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | spouse = [[Emperor Kanmu]] | house = [[Fujiwara clan]] (by birth)
{{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} (by marriage) | father = {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|en|Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|qid=Q11623505|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|1=Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu}} | mother = Abe no Komina | issue = {{plainlist| * {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}} * {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}} * Princess Koshi}} }} '''Fujiwara no Otomuro''' (藤原乙牟漏; ], 760 – April 28, 790) was a Japanese noblewoman and {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}}.Ponsonby-Fane, page 62. Her sister was {{ill|Fujiwara no Moroane|en|Fujiwara no Moroane|qid=Q5507531|lt=Fujiwara no Moroane|1=Fujiwara no Moroane}}. Fujiwara no Otomuro was a daughter of a noble called {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|en|Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|qid=Q11623505|lt=Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu|1=Fujiwara no Yoshitsugu}};Varley, p. 151; Brown, p. 279. her mother was the granddaughter of general {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|qid=Q1370638|lt=Fujiwara no Umakai|1=Fujiwara no Umakai}}, who died in 737.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Fujiwara no Umakai" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 211|page=211}}; Brinkley, FRank ''et al.'' (1915). {{Google books|JlUCAAAAYAAJ|''A History of the Japanese People from the Earliest Times to the End of the Meiji Era,'' p. 203.|page=203}} She married [[Emperor Kanmu]].''Religion in Japanese History'' by Joseph Mitsuo Kitagawa Their children included: *{{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}}[[H. Paul Varley|Varley, H. Paul.]] (1980). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ ''Jinnō Shōtōki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns.''] New York: Columbia University Press. {{ISBN|978-0-231-04940-5}}; [https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/59145842 OCLC 59145842] *{{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}Boroff, Nicholas. (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=LENNGgAACAAJ&q=national+geographic+traveler+japan ''National Geographic Traveler Japan.''] Washington, D.C.: National Geographic Society. She also had a daughter, Princess Koshi. Her daughter-in-law was Lady [[Tachibana no Kachiko]].Adolphson, Mikael S., {{ill|Edward Kamens|en|Edward Kamens|qid=Q5343880|lt=Edward Kamens|1=Edward Kamens}} and Stacie Matsumoto. (2006). [https://books.google.com/books?id=UjB8yiiGDykC&q=tachibana+no+kachiko ''Heian Japan, Centers and Peripheries.''] Honolulu: University of Hawaii Press. {{ISBN|9780824830137}}; [https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/71542885 OCLC 71542885] ==Notes== {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Inoe]] | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | after=[[Fujiwara no Taishi (died 794)|Fujiwara no Taishi]]
{{small|(granted title posthumously)}}| years=783–790}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Wikipedia:IPA for Japanese|[ɸu͍ʑiwaɽa no otomuɽo]] [[en:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[es:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو اوتومورو]] [[fr:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[id:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[ja:藤原乙牟漏]] [[no:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[sv:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[th:ฟูจิวาระ โนะ โอโตมูโระ]] [[tl:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[vi:Fujiwara no Otomuro]] [[zh:藤原乙牟漏]] {{wikidata link|Q2110461}} [[Category:760 births]] [[Category:790 deaths]] [[Category:8th-century empresses consort]] [[Category:8th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:8th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Emperor Kanmu]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
